items.title: GitHub webhook sync can leave link extraction permanently stale
items.author: patentsong
items.bodySnippet: **What happened?** `POST /webhooks/github` submits a `sync` Minion job without an explicit `noExtract` value. The standalone sync handler defaults missing `noExtract` to `true`: ```ts const noExtract = job.data.noExtract !== false; ``` The webhook job therefore imports the pushed commit and advances the source bookmark without materializing link/timeline edges. On the following autopilot source cycle, sync is alread…

items.title: Autopilot parent-child lock contention: child 'jobs work' process repeatedly fails to acquire parent's lock
items.author: deyil
items.bodySnippet: ## Summary When running `gbrain autopilot --repo <path>` via the LaunchAgent install, the autopilot process (PID 13444) spawns a child `gbrain jobs work --max-rss 8192` subprocess (PID 13531) to handle dispatched jobs. However, the child process attempts to acquire the same `autopilot.lock` file that the parent already holds, resulting in repeated lock contention errors logged to `~/.gbrain/autopilot.err`. This is d…

items.title: serve --http: steady RSS growth (~3 GB/day) under stateless Streamable-HTTP clients
items.author: daragao3
items.bodySnippet: ### Summary A long-running `gbrain serve --http` process grows its private memory roughly linearly at ~130 MB/h (~3 GB/day) while serving only loopback Streamable-HTTP MCP traffic. A fresh process starts at ~0.4–0.45 GB and reached 9.6 GB after ~2.5 days. Growth reproduces on every fresh process — we've watched several restart cycles re-grow at the same rate. items.bodySnippet: ### Summary `src/core/write-through.ts`'s atomic write path (`mkdirSync` → temp-write → `renameSync`) has no check for an existing file whose name differs from the target only in case. items.bodySnippet: **Thesis:** for visually-rich PDFs, "PDF → text → chunks" is the wrong abstraction. items.title: bug: `gbrain serve` as PID 1 doesn't reap orphaned subprocesses → zombie `git` accumulation → cgroup pids.max exhaustion → ~3–4 day crash-loop
items.author: TreyLawrence
items.bodySnippet: ## Summary On a containerized deployment, `gbrain serve` runs as **PID 1** and never reaps orphaned child processes. Subprocesses it (and its autopilot worker) spawn — chiefly `git` — get reparented to PID 1 when their parent is force-evicted, and because a Bun process running as PID 1 installs no `SIGCHLD` reaper, they accumulate as **zombies**. Over ~3–4 days they hit the cgroup `pids.max` ceiling, `fork()` starts…

items.title: fix(doctor): serialize onboard checks + add timeout to avoid connection-pool deadlock on remote engines
items.author: ElliotDrel
items.bodySnippet: ## Problem `runAllOnboardChecks` (`src/core/onboard/checks.ts`) runs its 7 DB checks concurrently via `Promise.all`. Each check acquires at least one connection, so on a pooled remote engine (Postgres/Supabase) with a small pool (e.g. `GBRAIN_POOL_SIZE=2`), the concurrent acquisitions exhaust the pool and deadlock — `gbrain doctor` hangs indefinitely (observed >20 min vs ~10s after the fix). It's invisible on PGLite…

items.title: OpenAI-compatible providers with default thinking (e.g. DeepSeek) waste ~50% tokens on non-reasoning calls
items.author: justemu
items.bodySnippet: ## Problem Several OpenAI-compatible providers, notably **DeepSeek v4-flash**, default to thinking/reasoning mode. When gbrain uses these models for non-reasoning calls (embeddings, search expansion, linting, etc.), every response carries unnecessary thinking tokens, inflating token costs by roughly 50%. items.title: parseFlag silently accepts unknown flags (e.g. --dry-run on init --migrate-only no-ops and applies real migrations)
items.author: ethanbeard
items.bodySnippet: ## Summary `parseFlag` resolves flags with a naive `args.indexOf(flag)`, which returns `-1` cleanly for any flag the function doesn't know about. Unknown flags are therefore silently accepted and ignored — no warning, no error, non-zero exit. This is a foot-gun for safety-critical flags that a caller assumes will prevent side effects. ## Concrete incident ``` gbrain init --migrate-only --dry-run ``` This applied rea…

items.title: gbrain-base-v2 drops meeting/conversation but engine treats them as first-class fact-extraction types
items.author: ethanbeard
items.bodySnippet: ## Summary `gbrain-base-v2` omits `meeting` and `conversation` from `page_types`, but the engine treats both as first-class, fact-extractable conversation types. items.author: ChenyqThu
items.bodySnippet: ## Summary Pages that yield **zero atoms** are rediscovered by `extract_atoms` forever. items.author: Topture
items.bodySnippet: ## Summary In `src/core/cycle/propose-takes.ts`, the idempotency cache row for a scanned page is only written as a side effect of inserting proposal rows into `take_proposals`. items.author: victorfteha

items.bodySnippet: ## Summary Closes #1946. `embedAllStale` armed the budget timer with `setTimeout(..., Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER)` when `--catch-up` was set. items.title: bug fix: multiple proposals for a page are silently dropped after extraction

Make it non-unique (migration 114 + fresh-install schema) and drop the INSERT's ON CONFLICT clause.

On a local PGLite brain each read is sub-millisecond, so this is invisible. On a **remote Postgres / Supabase pooler** each re…

items.title: fix(embed): per-batch token cap for OpenAI + opt-in for LiteLLM
items.author: kkroo
items.bodySnippet: Closes #1080. The gateway pre-split was gated on a recipe declaring `max_batch_tokens`. Three changes: 1. `recipes/openai.ts`: declare `max_batch_tokens: 300_000`, `chars_per_t…

EXECUTE '...' END $$` block. Postgres rejects `CONCURRENTLY` inside *any* function context — including statements run via `EXECUTE` in a `DO` block. So when the EXISTS guard triggers (because a prior `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` left an `indisvalid = f…

items.title: gbrain query --no-expand silently returns 'No results' without OPENAI_API_KEY
items.author: abolnasr
items.bodySnippet: The `--no-expand` flag on `gbrain query` is documented as disabling LLM-driven query expansion. It does NOT disable query-string EMBEDDING (used for pgvector similarity ranking). Without `OPENAI_API_KEY` in the environment, the embedding step fails silently. items.title: chunker_version config key missing — blocks chunker-aware reindex decisions
items.author: abolnasr
items.bodySnippet: Consumers of `gbrain reindex-code` benefit from knowing the version of the chunker used to produce existing embeddings. If the chunker upgrades (different chunk boundaries, different metadata), prior embeddings may need reindexing. The natural surface is: ```bash gbrain config get chunker_version ``` In v0.32, this returns `key not found` regardless of the running chunker version. The chunker IS versioned internally…

items.title: serve: terminal-launched processes accumulate across sessions, exhaust Supabase session pool over days
items.author: colin477
items.bodySnippet: ## Summary Each invocation of \`gbrain serve\` from an interactive terminal spawns a long-lived bun process that holds its own Postgres client pool. When the user closes the parent terminal (iTerm pane, tmux pane, VS Code integrated terminal, etc.) without explicitly \`Ctrl-C\`-ing or \`exit\`-ing first, **the serve process is not killed** — it orphans and stays alive indefinitely, still holding open Postgres connec…

On repos with large gitignored trees this bloats the DB, …

The question is narrower: What is already solid for self-hosted shared use, what is still local-first by design, what is contradictory in the docs/code, and what small improv…

items.title: Proposal: identity schema as runtime specification layer above AGENTS.md
items.author: Airmax1986
items.bodySnippet: GBrain solves retrieval at scale. Compiled truth is the right pattern for it. Compiled truth works beautifully at the document level. "Current best understanding of topic X." But there's a layer above that which is harder to compile: the subject itself. 10,000 files capture what someone has said, thought, built. They don't capture who that person is in a form a model can act on consistently. Garry Tan 2019 and Garry…

items.title: Feature request: Org-mode (.org) ingestion and sync support
items.author: guibor
items.bodySnippet: Hi, thanks for building gbrain. Request: support Org-mode (`.org`) files as an additional source format for `import` and `sync`. This seems like a strong fit for the project because there is a meaningful overlap between: - people using Org-mode - people maintaining large personal knowledge bases, research archives, and long-lived text repositories gbrain’s model of "repo as source of truth, indexed retrieval layer o…
